findings.reachability: Any logged-in WordPress user can POST/GET to admin-post.php?action=bsr_view_details. WordPress admin-post.php only requires a logged-in session for admin_post_ hooks (not nopriv), but there is no capability check and no nonce verification inside load_details() before it renders transient data.
findings.justification: The handler registered at line 127 of class-bsr-main.php (`admin_post_bsr_view_details`) calls `load_details()`, which performs zero authorization or CSRF checks — it only reads `bsr_results` transient and renders it. Any authenticated user (including Subscribers) can directly load the search/replace result details page.

detection_pattern.section_name: Broken Access Control: admin-post handler without auth check
detection_pattern.description: Matches admin_post_ action registrations. Files where the registered callback does not call check_admin_referer, wp_verify_nonce, or current_user_can are suspicious because any logged-in user can trigger admin-post.php handlers.

findings.reachability: Any logged-in WordPress user who can reach admin-post.php?action=bsr_view_details.
findings.justification: Because load_details() has no capability check, any subscriber-level user can view the bsr_results transient, which contains the search terms, replace terms, and table-level change counts from the most recent admin search/replace run. This leaks sensitive data about the site's database structure and recent modifications.

detection_pattern.section_name: Information Disclosure: transient data exposed without capability check
detection_pattern.description: Matches transient reads in handlers. When a handler reads and outputs transient data without checking user capabilities or nonces first, lower-privileged users may observe data from privileged operations.

findings.location: do_bsr_ajax() / BSR_AJAX::init() (class-bsr-ajax.php lines 70-81, registered on init hook)
findings.reachability: Any visitor (unauthenticated) can hit ?bsr-ajax=<action> on any page URL (the endpoint is admin_url/tools.php?...&bsr-ajax=, but the init hook fires on the front-end too if the query var is present). The dispatcher has no login check at the routing layer.
findings.justification: In do_bsr_ajax() (lines 70-81 of class-bsr-ajax.php), `do_action('bsr_ajax_' . $action)` is called unconditionally for any ?bsr-ajax= parameter during the init hook, before any authentication check. Individual handlers (e.g. process_search_replace) guard themselves with BSR_Utils::check_admin_referer, but the dispatch layer itself is fully open. Third-party code hooking bsr_ajax_* without its own auth check would be exposed.

detection_pattern.section_name: Unauthenticated dispatch: custom AJAX router without login gate
detection_pattern.description: Matches custom AJAX dispatch patterns that concatenate an action name and call do_action. If the routing layer lacks a login check, unauthenticated requests can trigger any hooked callback that does not have its own auth guard.
